content.text: Payment Methods

Azure payment methods are attached to the billing entity — the billing profile (MCA), the enrollment (EA), or the subscription (MOSP) — not to individual Azure subscriptions. When a payment method changes, all subscriptions billed through that entity are affected. Understanding payment method scoping is essential for enterprise billing governance and for automating expiry alerts and method rotation.

attrs.alt: Payment method types by Azure billing account type

MCA, MOSP
Billing profile (MCA) / Subscription (MOSP)
Charged at invoice date; card expiry = service risk
Wire Transfer / ACH
MCA (enterprise)
Billing profile
Net-30 terms; bank details provided on invoice
Check
MCA (select regions)
Billing profile
US only; 15-day clearing time
Prepay Commitment (Azure Credits)
EA
Enrollment
Paid upfront; overage billed as PAYG after credit depletion
MACC (Minimum Annual Commitment)
MCA enterprise
Billing profile
Contractual minimum; tracked against Azure Commit Report
Partner-managed
MPA (CSP)
Partner billing account
Reseller handles all payment; customer has no direct method
Listing Payment Methods via the Billing REST API

import requests
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

billing_account_name = "<billing-account-id>"
billing_profile_name = "<billing-profile-id>"

# MCA: list payment methods eligible for a billing profile
url = (
    f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
    f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
    f"billingProfiles/{billing_profile_name}/"
    f"paymentMethodLinks?api-version=2021-10-01"
)
resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
for pm in resp.json().get("value", []):
    p = pm.get("properties", {})
    print(
        f"Name: {pm['name']:<30}  "
        f"Type: {p.get('paymentMethodType'):<15}  "
        f"Status: {p.get('status')}"
    )

# List payment methods linked to an MCA billing profile
az billing payment-method list-by-billing-profile \
  --billing-account-name "<billing-account-id>" \
  --billing-profile-name "<billing-profile-id>" \
  --output table
Detecting Expiring Credit Cards

Credit cards associated with billing profiles expire silently. If the card is not updated before the invoice due date, Azure will retry the charge and eventually suspend the billing profile — stopping all new resource deployments.

import requests
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

def find_expiring_cards(billing_account_name: str, warn_days: int = 60) -> list:
    """
    Return payment methods expiring within warn_days days.
    Checks all billing profiles under the billing account.
    """
    credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
    token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
    auth = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

    # Get all billing profiles
    profiles_url = (
        f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
        f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/billingProfiles"
        f"?api-version=2020-05-01"
    )
    profiles_resp = requests.get(profiles_url, headers=auth)
    profiles = profiles_resp.json().get("value", [])

    expiring = []
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    for profile in profiles:
        profile_name = profile["name"]
        profile_display = profile["properties"]["displayName"]

        pm_url = (
            f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
            f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
            f"billingProfiles/{profile_name}/"
            f"paymentMethodLinks?api-version=2021-10-01"
        )
        pm_resp = requests.get(pm_url, headers=auth)
        for pm in pm_resp.json().get("value", []):
            props = pm.get("properties", {})
            if props.get("paymentMethodType") != "CreditCard":
                continue

            exp_month = props.get("expMonth")   # 1–12
            exp_year  = props.get("expYear")    # e.g. 2025
            if not (exp_month and exp_year):
                continue

            # Last second of expiry month
            import calendar
            last_day = calendar.monthrange(exp_year, exp_month)[1]
            exp_dt = datetime(exp_year, exp_month, last_day, 23, 59, 59, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
            days_left = (exp_dt - now).days

            if days_left <= warn_days:
                expiring.append({
                    "billing_profile": profile_display,
                    "payment_method": pm["name"],
                    "last_four": props.get("lastFour", "****"),
                    "exp_date": f"{exp_year}-{exp_month:02d}",
                    "days_until_expiry": days_left,
                    "status": props.get("status"),
                })

    return sorted(expiring, key=lambda x: x["days_until_expiry"])


for card in find_expiring_cards("<billing-account-id>", warn_days=90):
    print(
        f"Profile: {card['billing_profile']:<30}  "
        f"Card: *{card['last_four']}  "
        f"Expires: {card['exp_date']}  "
        f"Days left: {card['days_until_expiry']}"
    )
Updating a Payment Method on a Billing Profile

Payment method updates are done via the Azure Portal — the REST API does not expose a write endpoint for adding or replacing credit cards (for PCI compliance reasons). However, the API does allow unlinking a payment method from a billing profile and linking a different one that has already been saved to the billing account.

# Link a different (already-saved) payment method to a billing profile
az billing payment-method link \
  --billing-account-name "<billing-account-id>" \
  --billing-profile-name "<billing-profile-id>" \
  --payment-method-name "<payment-method-resource-name>"

# Unlink a payment method from a billing profile
az billing payment-method unlink \
  --billing-account-name "<billing-account-id>" \
  --billing-profile-name "<billing-profile-id>" \
  --payment-method-name "<payment-method-resource-name>"

import requests
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

def link_payment_method(
    billing_account_name: str,
    billing_profile_name: str,
    payment_method_name: str
) -> int:
    """
    Link an existing payment method (already saved to the billing account)
    to a billing profile. Returns HTTP status code.
    """
    credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
    token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {token}",
        "Content-Type": "application/json"
    }

    # The payment method resource ID is under the billing account, not the profile
    payment_method_id = (
        f"/providers/Microsoft.Billing/billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
        f"paymentMethods/{payment_method_name}"
    )

    url = (
        f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
        f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
        f"billingProfiles/{billing_profile_name}/"
        f"paymentMethodLinks/{payment_method_name}"
        f"?api-version=2021-10-01"
    )
    body = {"properties": {"paymentMethod": {"id": payment_method_id}}}
    resp = requests.put(url, headers=headers, json=body)
    print(f"Link status: {resp.status_code}")
    return resp.status_code

link_payment_method("<billing-account-id>", "<billing-profile-id>", "<payment-method-name>")
Monitoring Billing Profile Payment Failures

When a payment charge fails, Azure sets the billing profile status to `Warned` and retries over several days. If all retries fail, the status moves to `Disabled` and new resource deployments are blocked. Programmatically monitoring the profile status enables early intervention.

A billing profile with status 'Warned' still allows new deployments. 'Disabled' blocks all new resource creates. Resolve payment failures immediately — once disabled, re-enabling requires contacting Microsoft Billing support.
Status
Effect
Action Required
Active
Normal operation
None
Warned
Payment retrying; deployments allowed
Update payment method within 7 days
Disabled
New resource creates blocked; existing resources run
Contact Microsoft Billing support; update payment immediately
Deleted
Billing profile removed; subscriptions must be reassigned
Re-associate subscriptions with an active billing profile
Listing All Payment Methods at the Billing Account Level

Payment methods are saved at the billing account level and linked to billing profiles. A billing account can have multiple saved payment methods; each billing profile uses exactly one. Listing all saved methods gives the full inventory for rotation planning.

import requests
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

billing_account_name = "<billing-account-id>"

# List all payment methods saved to the billing account
url = (
    f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
    f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
    f"paymentMethods?api-version=2021-10-01"
)
resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
print(f"{'Name':<40} {'Type':<15} {'Last4':<8} {'Expiry':<8} {'Status'}")
print("-" * 90)
for pm in resp.json().get("value", []):
    props = pm.get("properties", {})
    expiry = ""
    if props.get("expMonth") and props.get("expYear"):
        expiry = f"{props['expYear']}-{props['expMonth']:02d}"
    print(
        f"{pm['name']:<40} "
        f"{props.get('paymentMethodType', ''):<15} "
        f"{props.get('lastFour', ''):<8} "
        f"{expiry:<8} "
        f"{props.get('status', '')}"
    )
Checking Which Billing Profiles Use Each Payment Method

import requests
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

def map_payment_methods_to_profiles(billing_account_name: str) -> dict:
    """
    Return a dict: payment_method_name -> list of billing profile display names.
    Useful for auditing which profiles will be affected by a card rotation.
    """
    credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
    token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

    # Gather all profiles
    profiles_url = (
        f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
        f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/billingProfiles"
        f"?api-version=2020-05-01"
    )
    profiles = requests.get(profiles_url, headers=headers).json().get("value", [])

    mapping: dict[str, list[str]] = {}

    for profile in profiles:
        profile_name = profile["name"]
        display = profile["properties"]["displayName"]

        pm_url = (
            f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
            f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
            f"billingProfiles/{profile_name}/"
            f"paymentMethodLinks?api-version=2021-10-01"
        )
        pm_resp = requests.get(pm_url, headers=headers)
        for link in pm_resp.json().get("value", []):
            pm_name = link["name"]
            mapping.setdefault(pm_name, []).append(display)

    return mapping


result = map_payment_methods_to_profiles("<billing-account-id>")
for pm_name, profiles in result.items():
    print(f"Payment Method: {pm_name}")
    for p in profiles:
        print(f"  → {p}")
Automating Payment Method Rotation

When a credit card is about to expire, finance teams rotate it by: (1) adding the new card in the Azure Portal, (2) linking the new card to all billing profiles that used the old one, (3) unlinking the old card. The following script automates steps 2 and 3, assuming the new card is already saved to the billing account.

import requests
import time
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

def rotate_payment_method(
    billing_account_name: str,
    old_payment_method_name: str,
    new_payment_method_name: str,
    dry_run: bool = True
) -> list:
    """
    For every billing profile linked to old_payment_method_name,
    link new_payment_method_name and unlink old_payment_method_name.
    Set dry_run=False to actually make the changes.
    """
    credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
    token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {token}",
        "Content-Type": "application/json"
    }

    # Find affected profiles
    profiles_url = (
        f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
        f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/billingProfiles"
        f"?api-version=2020-05-01"
    )
    profiles = requests.get(profiles_url, headers=headers).json().get("value", [])

    affected = []
    for profile in profiles:
        profile_name = profile["name"]
        display = profile["properties"]["displayName"]

        pm_url = (
            f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
            f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
            f"billingProfiles/{profile_name}/"
            f"paymentMethodLinks?api-version=2021-10-01"
        )
        links = requests.get(pm_url, headers=headers).json().get("value", [])
        pm_names = [lnk["name"] for lnk in links]

        if old_payment_method_name not in pm_names:
            continue

        affected.append({"profile_name": profile_name, "display": display})

        if dry_run:
            print(f"[DRY-RUN] Would rotate profile: {display}")
            continue

        new_pm_id = (
            f"/providers/Microsoft.Billing/billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
            f"paymentMethods/{new_payment_method_name}"
        )
        base = (
            f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
            f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
            f"billingProfiles/{profile_name}/paymentMethodLinks"
        )

        # 1. Link new card
        link_resp = requests.put(
            f"{base}/{new_payment_method_name}?api-version=2021-10-01",
            headers=headers,
            json={"properties": {"paymentMethod": {"id": new_pm_id}}}
        )
        print(f"Link new  [{link_resp.status_code}]: {display}")

        # 2. Unlink old card
        unlink_resp = requests.delete(
            f"{base}/{old_payment_method_name}?api-version=2021-10-01",
            headers=headers
        )
        print(f"Unlink old [{unlink_resp.status_code}]: {display}")
        time.sleep(1)

    return affected


# First run dry to see affected profiles
affected = rotate_payment_method(
    "<billing-account-id>",
    old_payment_method_name="old-visa-****1234",
    new_payment_method_name="new-visa-****5678",
    dry_run=True
)
print(f"\n{len(affected)} billing profiles would be rotated")

# Then run live
# rotate_payment_method("<billing-account-id>", "old-visa-****1234", "new-visa-****5678", dry_run=False)
Azure Policy: Enforce Wire Transfer for Production Profiles

Some organizations require production billing profiles to use wire transfer rather than credit cards, to avoid single-card failure risk. This is enforced via billing governance policies or manual review — Azure Policy does not govern billing resources directly. A governance script can audit and report non-compliant profiles.

import requests
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

def audit_production_payment_methods(
    billing_account_name: str,
    required_type: str = "WireTransfer",
    profile_tag_prefix: str = "prod"
) -> list:
    """
    Check that billing profiles whose display name starts with prod_tag_prefix
    use required_type payment method. Returns a list of non-compliant profiles.
    """
    credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
    token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

    profiles_url = (
        f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
        f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/billingProfiles"
        f"?api-version=2020-05-01"
    )
    profiles = requests.get(profiles_url, headers=headers).json().get("value", [])

    violations = []
    for profile in profiles:
        display = profile["properties"]["displayName"]
        if not display.lower().startswith(profile_tag_prefix.lower()):
            continue

        profile_name = profile["name"]
        pm_url = (
            f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
            f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
            f"billingProfiles/{profile_name}/"
            f"paymentMethodLinks?api-version=2021-10-01"
        )
        methods = requests.get(pm_url, headers=headers).json().get("value", [])
        method_types = [
            m["properties"].get("paymentMethodType") for m in methods
        ]

        if required_type not in method_types:
            violations.append({
                "profile": display,
                "name": profile_name,
                "actual_types": method_types,
                "required": required_type,
            })

    return violations


violations = audit_production_payment_methods("<billing-account-id>")
if violations:
    print("NON-COMPLIANT PRODUCTION BILLING PROFILES:")
    for v in violations:
        print(f"  {v['profile']}: has {v['actual_types']}, requires {v['required']}")
else:
    print("All production billing profiles use wire transfer.")

Billing resources are not governed by Azure Policy (they live outside the ARM resource hierarchy). Use scheduled automation scripts and Azure Monitor alert rules on Billing API events for compliance monitoring.
Automated Expiry Alert Using Azure Logic Apps or a Timer Function

The expiry monitoring pattern runs daily against the Billing API. A practical production implementation uses an Azure Function with a Timer trigger (or Logic App with a recurrence trigger) to check and send email alerts via the SendGrid or Azure Communication Services connector.

# Azure Function: TimerTrigger for payment method expiry alerts
# requirements.txt: azure-functions, azure-identity, requests, sendgrid

import logging
import azure.functions as func
import requests
import json
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

app = func.FunctionApp()

BILLING_ACCOUNT = "<billing-account-id>"
ALERT_EMAIL = "cloud-finance@contoso.com"
WARN_DAYS = 60

@app.timer_trigger(schedule="0 0 8 * * *", arg_name="timer")  # Daily at 08:00 UTC
def check_payment_expiry(timer: func.TimerRequest) -> None:
    credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
    token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

    profiles_url = (
        f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
        f"billingAccounts/{BILLING_ACCOUNT}/billingProfiles?api-version=2020-05-01"
    )
    profiles = requests.get(profiles_url, headers=headers).json().get("value", [])
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    warnings = []

    for profile in profiles:
        pm_url = (
            f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
            f"billingAccounts/{BILLING_ACCOUNT}/"
            f"billingProfiles/{profile['name']}/paymentMethodLinks?api-version=2021-10-01"
        )
        methods = requests.get(pm_url, headers=headers).json().get("value", [])
        for pm in methods:
            props = pm.get("properties", {})
            exp_m = props.get("expMonth")
            exp_y = props.get("expYear")
            if not (exp_m and exp_y and props.get("paymentMethodType") == "CreditCard"):
                continue
            import calendar
            last = calendar.monthrange(exp_y, exp_m)[1]
            exp_dt = datetime(exp_y, exp_m, last, 23, 59, 59, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
            days_left = (exp_dt - now).days
            if days_left <= WARN_DAYS:
                warnings.append({
                    "profile": profile["properties"]["displayName"],
                    "last_four": props.get("lastFour", "****"),
                    "exp_date": f"{exp_y}-{exp_m:02d}",
                    "days": days_left,
                })

    if warnings:
        body = "The following Azure billing payment methods are expiring soon:\n\n"
        for w in warnings:
            body += f"  Profile: {w['profile']}  Card: *{w['last_four']}  Expires: {w['exp_date']}  Days left: {w['days']}\n"
        logging.warning(body)
        # Send email via Azure Communication Services or SendGrid here
    else:
        logging.info("No expiring payment methods found.")
Summary

Azure payment methods are attached to the billing entity (billing profile, enrollment, or subscription) rather than to individual Azure subscriptions. MCA billing profiles support credit cards, wire transfer, and check; EA enrollments use prepaid commitments. Monitoring card expiry dates programmatically (60–90 days ahead) and polling billing profile status for non-Active states are the two most important automations for preventing service disruption due to payment failures.
Payment methods are scoped to billing profiles (MCA), enrollments (EA), or subscriptions (MOSP)
Credit card expiry does not trigger automatic notifications — build your own expiry monitor
The REST API can link/unlink existing saved payment methods; adding new cards requires the portal (PCI compliance)
Billing profile status Warned → Disabled timeline is typically 7–14 days of retry failure
Programmatically audit all billing profiles for non-Active status as part of daily operational checks
